core_mechanism: a symmetry-preserving local discretization of a continuous first-order (Dirac-type) operator onto a lattice, where a topological no-go obstruction forces a minimum number of degenerate low-energy modes emerging with linear dispersion near band-touching points
brief_reason: The abstract constructs a strictly local lattice fermion action whose excitations obey a relativistic Dirac equation and whose species count (two) is fixed as the minimum permitted by 'no-go' theorems, i.e. a topological obstruction to discretizing a symmetric first-order operator while keeping an exact chiral symmetry.
